content: Diagnostic criteria for T2DM: Any one of the following confirms diagnosis: (1) HbA1c ≥6.5% using NGSP-certified method; (2) Fasting plasma glucose ≥126 mg/dL (≥7.0 mmol/L) after ≥8-hour fast; (3) 2-hour plasma glucose ≥200 mg/dL during 75g OGTT; (4) Random plasma glucose ≥200 mg/dL with classic hyperglycemia symptoms. Abnormal results should be confirmed with repeat testing unless unequivocal hyperglycemia present. Prediabetes defined as HbA1c 5.7-6.4%, fasting glucose 100-125 mg/dL, or 2-hour OGTT 140-199 mg/dL. Screening recommended for adults ≥35 years, or younger with BMI ≥25 kg/m² plus risk factors (family history, hypertension, dyslipidemia, physical inactivity, high-risk ethnicity, history of gestational diabetes). Annual screening for prediabetes; every 3 years if normal.
